2606.24504.title: On the Smallness of the Large Language Models Scaling Exponents
2606.24504.paper_summary: We discuss reasons why the scaling exponents of current Large Language Models (LLMs) applications are indicating an unsustainable regime in terms of energy resources. We further show that attributing the smallness of such exponents to a numerical bias due to the neglect of a non-zero value of the loss function in the limit of infinite data (``pedestal effect") does not remove the unsustainability issue. Finally, the effects of the smoothness (roughness) of the data on the scaling exponents is commented upon based on an analogy with phenomenological models of fluid turbulence.
2606.24504.paper_summary_zh: 我們討論當前大型語言模型（LLMs）應用的縮放指數顯示出在能源資源方面的不可持續性原因。<lb/>我們進一步表明，將這些指數的微小性歸因於由於忽略在無限數據極限下損失函數的非零值所造成的數值偏差（“基座效應”）並不能消除不可持續性問題。<lb/>最後，根據流體湍流的現象學模型類比，對數據的平滑性（粗糙性）對縮放指數的影響進行了評論。
2606.24504.author: Sauro Succi et.al.
2606.24504.authors: Sauro Succi, Peter V. Coveney, Alex Hansen
